purpose: Counterfactual perturbation audit used to avoid local optima and catch silent drift away from the user's original requirements.

perturbations.change: Reframe product as general English teaching system
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks exam-first promise and paid trust model

perturbations.change: Reduce physical space to favorite box plus sleep box
perturbations.expected_failure: destroys knowledge-map semantics and core differentiation

perturbations.change: Remove detailed box catalog and keep only library/group skeleton
perturbations.expected_failure: drops content team's knowledge decomposition asset

perturbations.change: Treat hint as standalone card type
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks card authoring model and user-specified hint behavior

perturbations.change: Treat audio as independent interaction family
perturbations.expected_failure: adds an interaction family the user explicitly rejected

perturbations.change: Require heavy self-rating on every card
perturbations.expected_failure: increases software cost and breaks fluent continuity

perturbations.change: Make free-after-trial equivalent to full experience
perturbations.expected_failure: weakens paywall structure and contradicts user input

perturbations.change: Return to prose-first active docs
perturbations.expected_failure: moves active truth away from agent-first structured harness

perturbations.change: Drop peek from visible card actions
perturbations.expected_failure: removes a user-confirmed low-cost learning action

perturbations.change: Shrink free-after-trial into a tiny demo instead of near-half normal learning
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks the intended free-to-paid value bridge

perturbations.change: Turn physical space into either arbitrary free drag or fully locked system
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks the partial-control model specified by the user

perturbations.change: Assume one adaptive UI can cover phone, tablet, and PC web without dedicated design
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks the explicit multi-surface product requirement

perturbations.change: Treat PC as a separate native desktop client or drop Web parity
perturbations.expected_failure: misstates the release matrix and priority model

perturbations.change: Require exact same-card cross-device resume as product truth
perturbations.expected_failure: over-constrains continuity beyond what the user asked for

perturbations.change: Allow membership to diverge across iOS, Android, and Web or make Web non-purchasable
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks unified entitlement and all-surface commerce requirement

perturbations.change: Make audio unavailable on Web or tie it to one interaction family
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks audio content parity and misstates audio's role

perturbations.change: Demote physical space below top-level entry on one or more release targets
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks the required entry structure across surfaces

perturbations.change: Allow guest learning before login
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks the required authentication gate before learning starts

perturbations.change: Use a different login method or start trial at install/registration time
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks the confirmed authentication and trial trigger policy

perturbations.change: Change top-level navigation across release targets or drop keyboard/mouse adaptation on non-touch surfaces
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks confirmed navigation consistency and non-touch input policy

perturbations.change: Autoplay audio or require front-side subtitles
perturbations.expected_failure: breaks confirmed audio presentation policy

remaining_risk: future edits might shrink box catalog if not validated
future edits might over-spec implementation details unless runtime boundaries stay minimal
